title: Taraxacum glaucanthum
intro:
There is no information available about the plant Taraxacum glaucanthum. However, based on the similarity in name, it is likely to be a variant of Taraxacum officinale, also known as the common dandelion. Taraxacum officinale is a perennial plant in the sunflower family known for its bright yellow flowers and edible leaves. 

The health benefits of Taraxacum officinale include its ability to act as a diuretic, reduce inflammation, and support liver and digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale include taraxasterol, taraxasterol acetate, and sesquiterpene lactones. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Taraxacum officinale include tea, tincture, and capsules, which are often used to support liver and kidney health, as well as to relieve digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
